sentences.text: Schools should teach practical skills because students need them in real life.
Traditional study is still useful because it builds strong basic knowledge.
A mixed method often works best, with both theory and practice.
Technology can save time and make information easy to get.
Too much media use can harm focus and healthy communication.
Technology is useful when people use it with clear limits.
Workers need fair hours so they can stay healthy and productive.
Some strict rules can reduce company flexibility in hard times.
Good policy should protect workers and support business growth.
Clear laws can guide behavior and protect vulnerable people.
Rules alone are not enough if people do not accept them.
Law and education should work together for long-term change.
Climate action should start now because delay makes problems worse.
Short-term costs are high, so policy needs careful planning.
Shared action by governments, companies and people is the best path.
Public transport can cut traffic and make cities more livable.
Rapid city changes can hurt local culture and small businesses.
Cities need modern development and protection of key heritage.
